turns.note: Defines x-tilde = x + sigma*eps with eps ~ N(0,I) but then gives the target as -eps/sigma^2 and calls it equivalent to (x - x-tilde)/sigma^2 — under that convention the notes' true score is -eps/sigma, and the two forms it equates differ by a factor of sigma; it also misquotes Section 2 of the cheat sheet.
Unweighted loss 1/2 ||S_theta + eps/sigma^2||^2 matches Section 3 exactly, and the trap warning (default to the basic Euclidean form when no lambda is mentioned) is reproduced.

Names lambda(sigma) = sigma^2 and gives the notes' exact weighted form ||sigma*S_theta + eps||^2, but falsely presents sigma^2*||S_theta + eps/sigma^2||^2 as 'equivalently (multiplying out)' the same expression — those two are not equal.

Presents 'the update rule from your cheat sheet' as x + alpha_i*S_theta + sqrt(2*alpha_i)*Z, but the notes' Formula 6 and trap warning require (alpha_i/2) on the score term and sqrt(alpha_i) on the noise term; it even quotes the trap warning and then visibly contradicts it ('no root, no division—wait, actually...').
DDPM-to-score conversion S_theta = -eps_theta/sqrt(1 - alpha-bar_t) matches Section 7 exactly, correct sign and denominator, no unselected diffusion-notes content.

Five questions cover exactly the Turn 1-5 topics and Q1/Q2/Q3/Q5 answers are supported by the notes, but Q4's answer key hard-codes the wrong ALD coefficients (alpha_i and sqrt(2*alpha_i)) into teacher-facing grading material.
Student version keeps the same five questions (MCQ options are not answers) with all answers stripped; teacher version remains as its own prior message.
Names 05_ncsn_score_based_models_notes.pdf as the sole source and accurately says all seven sections of that cheat sheet were used (the file has exactly seven sections).

highlight: Fazah misstates the two formulas the scenario exists to check — true score given as -eps/sigma^2 under the x + sigma*eps convention (notes: -eps/sigma) and the ALD step as alpha*s + sqrt(2*alpha)*z (notes: (alpha/2)*s + sqrt(alpha)*z) — and the wrong ALD form propagates into the quiz answer key.
